L1.area: Document rendering and content inclusion pipeline

L2.description: A privilege escalation vulnerability exists in the wiki platform's content inclusion mechanism. When user-authored content is rendered through a shared template, it executes with the privileges of the template author rather than the content author. This allows a low-privileged user to execute arbitrary scripts by crafting content that is processed through a privileged template, effectively bypassing authorization controls.

L3.area: Document rendering and content inclusion pipeline
L3.description: A privilege escalation vulnerability exists in the wiki platform's content inclusion mechanism. When user-authored content is rendered through a shared template, it executes with the privileges of the template author rather than the content author. This allows a low-privileged user to execute arbitrary scripts by crafting content that is processed through a privileged template, effectively bypassing authorization controls.

ground_truth.reason: The content inclusion directive in a shared class template does not enforce author-based execution context, allowing any user's content to execute with the template author's elevated privileges.
